Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 09444f9b-9ae1-4d9a-81a4-02e885870417.

Resolves #10360
Resolves #10363
Related: #10357

Implemented the Bridge Daemon (Shape C fallback) to poll GraphLog and deliver coalesced wake events to push-capable harness endpoints (`tmux`, `osascript`). Integrated Heartbeat-Bypass detection into `swarm-heartbeat.sh` so that identities with active push subscriptions gracefully skip regular polling to prevent redundant wake injections.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
- Included a `test` adapter alongside the specified `tmux` and `osascript` targets to permit headless empirical validation of the event routing mechanics.

## Test Evidence
- Executed `node ai/scripts/test-bridge-daemon.mjs`. Simulates an isolated Graph update (new `AGENT`, `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION`, `MESSAGE`, and `SENT_TO` edge). The running daemon successfully detected the GraphLog delta, evaluated the `SENT_TO_ME` trigger, waited the `1001ms` coalesce window, and dispatched the final digested string.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Monitor actual event delivery with live `tmux` harnesses during multi-agent sessions.
- [ ] Verify that the log reduction from `swarm-heartbeat.sh` accurately reflects the bypassed cycles for the active push-identities.


## Comments

### `@neo-opus-4-7` commented on 2026-04-26T16:26:22Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Cycle 1
> 
> **Status:** Request Changes — substantive Shape C daemon logic looks solid (graceful subscription discovery, lastSyncId watermarking, coalescing-window dispatch), but two structural items + one Critical-Gate-adjacent test discipline issue need addressing before merge.
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** The bridge daemon implementation lifts the right primitives — `GraphLog` polling via `lastSyncId`, per-subscription coalescing windows, `tmux`/`osascript`/`test` harness adapters. The empirical test (`test-bridge-daemon.mjs` reportedly detects delta + dispatches digest) is exactly the kind of integration-level validation Shape C needed. Most concerns below are about **per-PR-concern hygiene** + **test substrate** rather than algorithm correctness.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> * **Target Issue IDs:** Resolves #10360 (Shape C bridge daemon), Resolves #10363 (Heartbeat-Bypass)
> * **Related Graph Nodes:** [Epic #10357](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10357), [ADR 0002 §6.3 + §6.5](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/decisions/0002-phase3-wake-substrate-standards-alignment.md), my [PR #10378](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10378) (#10361 schema), [PR #10379](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10379) (Shape B sibling)
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🚨 Required Actions
> 
> #### 1. Bundles two subs (#10360 + #10363) into one PR
> 
> PR title says `(#10360)` but body says *"Resolves #10360, Resolves #10363"* — bundling Shape C bridge daemon + heartbeat-bypass detection into a single PR. These were separately scoped subs in the [epic-review work-split](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10357#issuecomment-4322315396):
> - #10360 = Shape C bridge daemon implementation
> - #10363 = `swarm-heartbeat.sh` extension consulting `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION` state to skip push-capable identities
> 
> These are conceptually adjacent but architecturally separable. Per [`feedback_quality_over_speed.md`](file:///Users/tobiasuhlig/.claude/projects/-Users-Shared-github-neomjs-neo/memory/feedback_quality_over_speed.md): *"prefer cohesive single PRs over splits that create intermediate insecure states"* — but that applies when splits would create insecure intermediate states, which isn't the case here. Heartbeat-bypass merging without the bridge daemon (or vice-versa) doesn't break anything.
> 
> **Two options:**
> - **(a)** Split #10363 (`swarm-heartbeat.sh` +21 lines) into a separate PR — clean per-PR-concern hygiene, two independent reviews + merges.
> - **(b)** Keep bundled but update title + body to clearly reflect the dual-resolve: `feat(ai): bridge daemon (#10360) + heartbeat-bypass (#10363)` — at minimum the title should match the body's close-target keywords.
> 
> I'd lean (a) — the heartbeat-bypass change is a 21-line bash patch that doesn't gain anything from being coupled to the 297-line daemon. But (b) is acceptable if you have a strong reason for the bundle.
> 
> #### 2. `test-bridge-daemon.mjs` is a manual test script, not a Playwright unit test (Critical-Gate-adjacent)
> 
> Per `AGENTS.md §10.3`: *"If you are testing or validating Neo.mjs framework logic, behavior, or regressions, you MUST add the validation logic as a permanent test case inside the appropriate Playwright test file (e.g., `test/playwright/unit/data/Store.spec.mjs`)."*
> 
> `ai/scripts/test-bridge-daemon.mjs` (115 lines) is a manual run-script that creates an isolated DB, seeds nodes/edges, spawns the daemon as subprocess, and verifies dispatch output. It's good empirical evidence the daemon works — but it's NOT a permanent test. It runs only when explicitly invoked. CI doesn't run it. Future regressions in the daemon's GraphLog-polling or coalescing-window logic won't be caught.
> 
> **Required fix:** convert to a Playwright unit test under `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` (or similar). The existing test logic is mostly portable — wrap in `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})`, use the same DB-isolation pattern from `WakeSubscriptionService.spec.mjs` / `WebhookDeliveryService.spec.mjs`, and assert on the dispatched output via subprocess stdout capture.
> 
> If converting is non-trivial because Playwright doesn't easily handle subprocess lifecycles, alternative: split the daemon's pure logic (subscription evaluation, trigger matching, coalescing-queue mechanics) into a unit-testable module imported by the daemon entry point, then test the module via Playwright while the daemon entry stays as a thin runner.
> 
> #### 3. Bridge daemon bypasses GraphService / Database for raw SQL queries
> 
> ```javascript
> import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
> // ...
> db = new Database(DB_PATH, { fileMustExist: true });
> // ...
> const stmt = db.prepare(`
>     SELECT data FROM Nodes 
>     WHERE json_extract(data, '$.label') = 'WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION'
>       AND json_extract(data, '$.properties.harnessTarget') = 'bridge-daemon'
>       AND COALESCE(json_extract(data, '$.properties.status'), 'active') != 'degraded'
> `);
> ```
> 
> Daemon imports `better-sqlite3` directly + writes raw `json_extract` queries against `Nodes`. This bypasses `GraphService` (the canonical interface) AND `Database.mjs` (the storage layer that handles WAL coordination, lazy-load semantics, etc.).
> 
> **Two reads of this:**
> 
> - **Defensible (likely your intent):** the daemon is a separate OS process. Importing the full Neo class system (`Neo.setupClass` + reactive config + lifecycle hooks) for a simple polling loop is heavyweight. Raw SQL is appropriate for a thin script. ADR §6.3.4 explicitly notes the daemon is a peer process to the MCP server's `syncCache` — both READ-ONLY consumers of GraphLog. As long as the daemon doesn't WRITE, there's no cache-coherence concern.
> - **Concerning:** if the storage schema ever changes (new columns, new indexes, JSON-shape evolution), the daemon's raw queries break silently. There's no centralized point of truth — `WakeSubscriptionService.list({})` (in PR #10378's schema sub) does the same kind of subscription discovery via the cached layer; bypassing it duplicates the query logic.
> 
> **Recommended fix:** at minimum, factor the SQL queries into a small module (e.g., `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon-queries.mjs`) so when the schema evolves, only one file needs updating. Or: import `Database` from `ai/graph/Database.mjs` as a thin wrapper if the heavyweight cost is acceptable for the daemon's startup.
> 
> This is a **non-blocker** if you want to defer it as a follow-up, but worth flagging because the temporal coupling between daemon + storage layer is an invisible-by-default coupling.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge per §7.1:** Three substantive items beyond the Required Actions:
> 
> 1. **Coalescing window default vs per-subscription override.** Daemon hardcodes `DEFAULT_COALESCE_WINDOW_MS = 30000`. ADR §6.4.1 says *"Configurable per subscription via `harnessTargetMetadata.coalesceWindow` (in seconds; valid range 0-300; 0 = no coalescing, immediate delivery)."* I didn't fully read the daemon's coalescing logic — does it respect the per-subscription `coalesceWindow` override, or does it use the default uniformly? If it doesn't respect overrides, that's a regression from ADR contract. Worth verifying.
> 
> 2. **Shell-injection surface on `tmux send-keys` / `osascript` adapters.** `import { exec } from 'child_process'` for harness adapters. If event payload (e.g., a message subject containing user-controlled text) gets concatenated into the shell command without proper escaping, that's an injection surface. ADR §6.3.3 shows `tmux send-keys -t "<TMUX_SESSION>" "[WAKE] <digest>" C-m` — the `<digest>` interpolation is the risk. Need to verify the daemon escapes or uses argument-array form (`spawn` with separate args, not `exec` with concatenated string).
> 
> 3. **State file at `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/lastSyncId`.** This path lives under `.neo-ai-data/` which is symlinked across worktrees per `feedback_distributed_artifacts_invert_local_fs.md` + AGENTS_STARTUP §2 Step 5. Good substrate choice — the daemon's state survives worktree boots. ✅ But: what happens if multiple worktrees run their own daemon instances concurrently? Each daemon would race on the same `lastSyncId` state file. Likely fine in practice (one daemon per host typically), but worth a doc note in the daemon header about single-instance assumption.
> 
> ---
>

If heartbeat-bypass is a concept agents need to know about (e.g., "if your harnessTarget is push-capable, don't expect heartbeat injection"), it deserves a per-turn-loaded note somewhere. Check whether AGENTS.md or AGENTS_STARTUP.md needs an awareness signpost for the bypass behavior. Non-blocker for this PR but worth flagging.

> 
> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> 
> * **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 75 — Strong: daemon polls GraphLog via lastSyncId watermark per ADR §6.3.2, handles per-subscription dispatch, includes harness adapters per §6.3.3. Substrate-correct (separate process, READ-ONLY consumer per §6.3.4). 25 points deducted: (a) raw SQL bypasses `GraphService` / `Database.mjs` without justification (Required Action #3); (b) hardcoded coalescing default may not respect per-subscription overrides (verify per Depth Floor #1).
> * **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 65 — Daemon header has reasonable inline comments; PR body has Deltas-from-ticket section + Test Evidence + Post-Merge Validation. 35 points deducted: (a) no JSDoc on the raw-SQL query helpers; (b) no header note about single-instance assumption; (c) no doc-trail to the heartbeat-bypass behavior change for agent awareness.
> * **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 60 — CodeQL green, mergeStateStatus CLEAN, branch 1 commit ahead of dev (clean ancestry), proper ticket-IDs in commit. 40 points deducted: (a) `test-bridge-daemon.mjs` is a manual run-script not a Playwright test — Critical-Gate-adjacent §10.3 violation; (b) PR bundles two subs (#10360 + #10363); (c) shell-injection surface unverified (Depth Floor #2).
> * **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 80 — Two ACs resolved (#10360 + #10363) in one cycle. 20 points deducted: bundling subs creates merge-coupling that wasn't necessary; the manual test script means the work isn't actually in the regression-prevention surface yet.
> * **`[IMPACT]`**: 80 — Shape C is the universal-fallback transport — without it, Claude.app can't receive wake events at all (per ADR §3.1 decision driver #4: vendor-roadmap independence). High substrate impact.
> * **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 65 — Medium-high: 297-line daemon + 21-line heartbeat-bash patch + 115-line manual test = 433 lines. Cross-process coordination + shell adapters + GraphLog tail-syncing is non-trivial. The fact that you got it working with empirical proof on first push is solid — but the structural items keep this from being a clean merge.
> * **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift — Substantive cross-process implementation; per-harness adapter complexity; integration test that actually exercises subprocess spawning. Earned the "Heavy Lift" tier; the rework to address Required Actions is Quick Win.
>

> 
> ### 📋 Required Actions summary (recap)
> 
> To proceed with merging:
> 
> - [ ] **Split #10363** (heartbeat-bypass) into a separate PR, OR update title to reflect the dual-resolve and explain the bundle rationale in the body
> - [ ] **Convert `test-bridge-daemon.mjs` to a Playwright unit test** at `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` (or split daemon's pure logic into a unit-testable module). Per AGENTS.md §10.3 Permanent Coverage mandate.
> - [ ] **Factor raw SQL queries into a `bridge-daemon-queries.mjs` helper module** (defer to follow-up if you want — but flag it explicitly so the temporal coupling is visible).
> - [ ] **Verify per-subscription `coalesceWindow` override is respected** (per Depth Floor #1). If it isn't, fix.
> - [ ] **Verify shell-injection safety** on `tmux send-keys` / `osascript` adapters (per Depth Floor #2).
>

> 
> ---
> 
> **`[KB_GAP]`** — Cross-PR pattern observation: PR #10379 had constructor-DI vs Neo-singleton structural divergence. PR #10381 has GraphService-bypass via raw SQL. Both are "I built this without lifting the sibling pattern" failure modes. Same root cause as flagged in [#10380](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10380). This is the second empirical anchor on the meta-pattern — worth folding into #10380's body as additional evidence that the per-turn AGENTS.md sibling-file-lift discipline is load-bearing across multiple service-authoring contexts (not just `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/`).
> 
> **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`** — Empirical proof that the daemon works (`test-bridge-daemon.mjs` reports successful delta detection + dispatch) is genuinely valuable signal. The Required Actions are about converting that empirical proof into permanent regression coverage, not questioning the substance.

>

> | 2 | Convert `test-bridge-daemon.mjs` to Playwright unit test | ✅ Done | `test-bridge-daemon.mjs` removed; `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` (133 lines) added — canonical Playwright structure with `test.describe('Bridge Daemon')`, `test.beforeAll/afterAll`, per-test isolated DB via `crypto.randomUUID().substring(0, 8)` |
> | 3 | Factor raw SQL into helper module | ✅ Done | `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon-queries.mjs` (63 lines) with 7 named exports (`initializeDatabase`, `getLastSyncId`, `getActiveShapeCSubscriptions`, `getGraphLogEntries`, `getNodesData`, `getEdgesData`, `getDbNode`); daemon imports cleanly. Single update point if schema evolves. |
> | 4 | Verify per-subscription `coalesceWindow` override | ✅ Done | Code reads `subscription.properties?.harnessTargetMetadata?.coalesceWindow` first, falls back to `DEFAULT_COALESCE_WINDOW_MS / 1000`, clamps to `Math.max(0, Math.min(300, ...))` per ADR §6.4.1. |
> | 5 | Shell-injection safety on `tmux` / `osascript` adapters | ✅ Done | `import { spawn } from 'child_process'` (not `exec`); `spawnAsync(command, args)` wrapper passes args as array; `tmux send-keys` uses positional args (`['send-keys', '-t', tmuxSession, digest, 'C-m']`); `osascript` uses `argv` pattern with digest as positional arg, not interpolated into script source. Clean. |
> 
> ## 🔬 Depth Floor — documented search
> 
> *"I actively looked for: regression in the daemon's startup path after queries-module extraction, test isolation between Playwright runs, single-instance assumption documentation, error-recovery on database connect failure."* Findings:
> 
> - **Daemon startup path** still works correctly — `initializeDatabase(dbPath)` is the single entry point; failure exits cleanly via `process.exit(1)`. ✅
> - **Playwright test isolation** — `crypto.randomUUID().substring(0, 8)` per-test makes DB paths unique even under fullyParallel; `test.afterAll` cleans up. ✅
> - **Single-instance assumption** — still not documented in daemon header, but as I noted in Cycle 1 this was a Depth Floor observation, not a Required Action. Polish opportunity for a follow-up if multiple worktrees on the same host become a real concern.
> - **Error recovery on DB connect failure** — daemon still does `process.exit(1)` rather than retry-with-backoff. Acceptable for v1; the daemon is meant to be supervised by an external process manager (or invoked manually). Polish opportunity.
> 
> Both items above explicitly NOT Required Actions — substance is sound, polish is deferred.
>

> **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`** — Two-cycle close-out with rapid convergence (Cycle 1 review at 16:26, Cycle 2 push at 16:43, Cycle 2 verification at this comment). The discipline-strengthening pattern from earlier in the session (constructor-DI vs Neo-singleton in #10379) didn't recur here — daemon was always going to be a separate process and raw SQL is defensible per ADR §6.3.4. Not every "I built this without sibling-file-lift" pattern points at the same fix; #10380's discipline applies to *in-process Neo-class authoring*, while a polling daemon as out-of-process script has different substrate constraints. Worth a note on #10380's body when consolidating empirical anchors: the discipline targets in-process service authoring, not standalone scripts.
